LRM-130: Replace ACF with custom WP Admin homepage settings page

Root cause: PHP-local acf_add_local_field_group() creates a 'php local' group
that ACF's Gutenberg REST API integration never renders — fields are invisible
in the page editor regardless of location rules or DB entries.

Solution: drop ACF field registration, implement a dedicated admin settings page:

- inc/admin-homepage.php: submenu page under Pages → "Mājaslapa — saturs"
  - Language tabs: LV / EN / SV (each tab edits its own wp_options entry)
  - Sections: Hero, Stats (repeater), About (wysiwyg), Services (repeater), CTA
  - WP Media Library picker for image fields
  - JS repeater (add/remove rows) for stats and service cards
  - Values saved to vv_homepage_{lang} via update_option()
  - Provides vv_hp() and vv_hp_rows() template helpers

- inc/acf-fields.php: emptied (tombstone comment only)
- functions.php: requires admin-homepage.php, removes old vv_field()
- front-page.php: all content via vv_hp() / vv_hp_rows() with vv_t() fallbacks
- footer.php: CTA strip via vv_hp() with vv_t() fallbacks
- Deleted empty DB ACF field group posts 949-953

Page is fully backward-compatible: vv_t() fallbacks render until fields saved.

commit.message:
Fix renderPages: replace PDFKit with CGPDFDocument for thread-safe rendering

PDFKit (PDFDocument/PDFPage) is not reliably usable off the main thread on
iOS — page.pageRef returns nil and draw calls are silently skipped, leaving
all pages white. Replace with pure CoreGraphics CGPDFDocument/CGPDFPage,
which is fully thread-safe and works correctly on @DocRenderActor.

Also removes the PDFKit import; only CoreGraphics is now required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add report: early-exit vectorization is overly conservative

LLVM refuses to vectorize find-first loops citing "potentially faulting
load" even when loop bounds guarantee safety.

Key findings:
- The load is NOT actually faulting - all accesses within [0, n)
- LLVM conflates "speculative read past early exit" with "read past bounds"
- Ternary reformulation vectorizes but loses early exit
- RVV has vle*ff.v (fault-first) and vfirst.m for this exact pattern
- LLVM's vectorizer has vleff intrinsics but doesn't auto-generate them

This is a missed optimization opportunity for search/find patterns.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix video duration: put --duration flag FIRST in Seedance prompt

Root cause: long scene prompts (400+ chars from Claude enhancement)
caused --duration flag at the END to be truncated by Seedance API,
defaulting to 5s per segment instead of 10s. Result: 13s instead of 30s.

Fix:
- Move --duration, --ratio, --camerafixed to START of prompt text
- Truncate descriptive prompt to 400 chars max
- Add total duration validation (pad missing segments if needed)
- Add debug logging for prompt length and duration
- Applied to all 3 routes: t2v, i2v, video-long

commit.message:
Replace CoinGecko with fully on-chain DEX price tracking

Token prices now read directly from DEX pair reserves — works for any
token including newly launched ones with no CoinGecko listing.

Price derivation:
- Native USD (BNB/ETH): read USDT/WBNB or USDC/WETH pair reserves
- Token USD: find token/WNATIVE pair → price in native → × native USD
- Tries all configured DEX factories per chain until a pair is found

Removes all external HTTP price API dependencies. Prices update every
15 minutes using public RPC calls only.

commit.message:
Fix build: add index.src.html template and vite plugin to manage index.html

Problem: index.html was pointing to built dist assets, causing Vite
to fail on subsequent builds (Rollup couldn't resolve the hashed asset paths).

Solution:
- index.src.html: permanent source template referencing src/main.jsx
- vite.config.js: custom plugin with buildStart (restores index.src.html ->
  index.html before build) and closeBundle (copies dist/index.html -> index.html
  after build for server deployment)

npm install && npm run build now works cleanly every time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: supervisor mission-complete detection (5 strategies) (#1869)

* fix: supervisor mission-complete detection (5 strategies)

A. Deterministic mission-complete fallback — after LLM actions, auto-
   declare complete if 0 open issues, 0 open PRs, and 2+ recently-
   closed issues resolved by review. Safety net for when the LLM fails
   to recognise completion.

B. Fix first-run-after-init detection — check actionsSinceInit for
   prior supervisor entries instead of recentActivity string match.
   Stops every run from incorrectly announcing as first run.

C. Fix bot dispatch inputs — agentic-lib-bot.yml only accepts
   discussion-url, not message. Remove invalid message input from
   dispatchBot() and respond:discussions handler.

D. Prompt restructuring — add mandatory first check to agent-supervisor
   prompt: evaluate mission completion before choosing any action.

E. Source exports in supervisor context — scan source files for exported
   functions and include in the prompt so the LLM has direct evidence
   of what's implemented.

Also adds BENCHMARK_REPORT_002.md documenting the hamming-distance
scenario where all acceptance criteria pass but mission-complete was
never declared by the LLM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(C): add message input to bot workflow and wire through

Instead of removing the message from dispatchBot(), add `message` as
an accepted input to agentic-lib-bot.yml (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch).
The message flows through as BOT_MESSAGE env var to the discussions task,
which includes it in the LLM prompt as "Triggering Request" — identifying
it as the primary request to respond to.

Also wire INPUT_MESSAGE from workflow params to the supervisor step in
agentic-lib-workflow.yml so the auto-respond logic has the message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 4: REST API + n8n integration

Auth:
- ApiToken entity + migration (api_tokens table, linked to site_id)
- ApiTokenAuthenticator: DB token lookup, sets SiteContext + enables
  SiteFilter — no longer relies on Host header for API requests
- ApiUser: stores siteId from token
- SiteResolverSubscriber: skip /api/ paths (auth handles site context)
- CreateApiTokenCommand: generate tokens via CLI

REST API (/api/v1/*):
- ArticleApiController: GET list (filters: status/category/is_ai_generated/
  older_than), POST create, GET show, PUT update, PATCH publish
- ContentQueueApiController: GET list by status, POST create, PATCH update
- SiteApiController: GET list
- CategoryApiController: GET list (site-filtered)
- MediaApiController: POST upload (delegates to MediaManager)
- ToolApiController: POST /suggest-links (uses InternalLinker)
- AbstractApiController: success/error/paginated/decodeBody helpers

Repositories:
- ArticleRepository: findForApi() with flexible filters, findBySlugUnfiltered()
- ContentQueueRepository: findByStatus()
- TagRepository: findBySlug()

n8n Workflows (n8n-workflows/):
- topic-discovery.json: Cron 4h → RSS feeds → deduplicate → POST /queue
- content-generation.json: Cron 30m → GET queue → Claude claude-opus-4-6 →
  POST /articles (draft) → PATCH queue completed
- post-processing.json: Cron 1h → GET drafts → suggest-links → Claude meta
  → PUT article → PATCH publish
- content-refresh.json: Weekly → GET old evergreen → Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 →
  PUT article

Prompt templates (config/prompts/{vertical}/):
- crypto/news_article.yaml: journalist tone, TL;DR, data points, 1500w
- finance/news_article.yaml: educational, key takeaways, 1800w
- gambling/news_article.yaml: responsible gambling, strategy, 1200w
